title: Fix Docker APIv2 container wait endpoint
body:
resolves #8697
resolves #9157
* container wait functions are now cancel-able via `context.Context`
* container wait functions can wait for multiple states, not just single one
* docker container wait endpoint now accepts docker conditions: `not-running`, `removed`, `next-exit`
* docker container wait endpoint now sends headers in advance before actual waiting

comments.body: @mheon PTAL
@rhatdan I think this conflicts with some of the work you've been doing, and I think I prefer this version - it enables more functionality that we don't have yet.

I disagree, I am fine with most of this, but it should all be implemented in the standard API code.  We should not have three different ways of doing the same function.  Too much risk of them drifting, as I have already seen with every single interface I have looked at.
@rhatdan do you mean I should add the three new condition to the `define.ContainerStatus` `"enum"` and then handle it in `WaitForConditionWithInterval()` ? What you are working on?

@rhatdan I disagree. Our API should not support the Docker-specific constants or the specifics of their operation mode. We can try and unify these but it's going to be a massive pain of conditional code

My point being that podman-remote wait and podman wait should ultimately call into pkg/domain/infra/abi/ wait function.
If Docker API needs some fancy handling, I am fine with adding it.
I would like to get this merged along with the Stop/Remove/Wait patch that I have going into todays release.

@rhatdan I don't believe the way Docker's Wait and our Wait work are compatible at this point. The handling for the custom condition codes is fundamentally different from the way our Wait code functions.

I think that really different is `next-exit` condition. Seems to me that in `podman` `condition == state`. This is not true for `next-exit` since it describes _transition_ between two states instead of one particular state.

Condition `not-running` is not that different what `podman` has. As for `removed`, there is no such a state since the container no longer exists :smile: ,  so I am basically waiting for `define.ErrNoSuchCtr`.
Note that `next-exit` may fail for short living containers (even if polling interval is 1ns).
I did experiment with condition variables it the shared memory as replacement for polling, but results weren't satisfactory.
To make `next-exit` truly reliable we probably need to introduce some better inter-process notification mechanism. Any ideas what we could use?

Let's get https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9051 in and then rebase this on it.
Please review ^^
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *matejvasek*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@rhatdan I'll look at it and then rebase.

@mheon 
Given the recent rework -- calling `abi.ContainerEngine` instead of direct call to `Wait*()` on container,
should I change
```go
type WaitOptions struct {
	Condition define.ContainerStatus
	Interval  time.Duration
	Latest    bool
}
```
into
```go
type WaitOptions struct {
	Conditions []define.ContainerStatus
	Interval   time.Duration
	Latest     bool
}
```
or should I prefer handling waiting for multiple states in handler code?
That change LGTM
@matejvasek Needs a rebase.
@mheon @rhatdan PTAL

Hmm there is some problem with having `Conditions` as a slice.
```
• Failure [1.761 seconds]
Podman containers 
/home/mvasek/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/pkg/bindings/test/containers_test.go:18
  podman pause a paused container by name [It]
  /home/mvasek/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/pkg/bindings/test/containers_test.go:118

  Expected
      <*errors.fundamental | 0xc0006b6060>: {                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
          msg: "unknown slice type slice",                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
          stack: [0xec148d, 0xead31f, 0xf64849, 0xf6fa05, 0xec7563, 0xec7177, 0xec6587, 0xecd6d1, 0xecce12, 0xef4951, 0xef4467, 0xef3c77, 0xef6446, 0xf009b8, 0xf00608, 0xfa6a31, 0x530e2f, 0x478ae1],                                                                        
      }                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  to be nil 
```

IMO it could work not only for `string` but also for any type implementing `stringer` interface.

Edit:
Also it probably should work for integral types.

@rhatdan @mheon I am not sure if I understand all of this: Docker endpoint shall accept params as strings (e.g. "not-running"), and `/libpod` endpoints shall accept numerical values (enum int val)? Is that correct?
This needs some more work, not really ready for review.
I create PR for fixing the generator https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9203
/hold
The commit `[DO NOT MERGE] Improve generator` must be removed before merging + code has to be merged after https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9203 is merged.
I added `[DO NOT MERGE] Improve generator` only temporarily to see if CI pass.
This is odd, APIv2 tests works for me.
Some tests seems to fail randomly.
We will also need to backport https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9203
@rhatdan @mheon PTAL
@riyad I think you know how to run some docker conformance tests, could you please run it against my `/containers/{id}/wait`, or give me some pointers how to do it myself?
Done reviewing. @rhatdan @jwhonce PTAL
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

Great work @matejvasek Thanks.
/lgtm
/hold cancel
